skills.name: Address Validation Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when a developer needs to standardize, correct, and validate postal addresses globally before checkout or shipping. The Address Validation API processes an input address, suggests necessary fixes, and returns a geocoded, USPS CASS-certified (for US addresses) address with specific delivery details, ensuring higher delivery success rates.

skills.name: Aerial View Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when you need to fetch the URL for a pre-rendered, photorealistic 3D flyover video of a location, or retrieve the necessary metadata for embedding or displaying the aerial view provided by Google Maps Platform.

skills.name: Agentic Ui Toolkit Javascript
skills.description: Provides canonical JavaScript solutions for Google Maps Platform, focusing on modern, cost-optimized APIs for interactive mapping, place details (`Place.fetchFields`), routes (`Route.computeRoutes`), and visual enhancements (`AdvancedMarkerElement`), while strictly enforcing migration from all deprecated APIs.

skills.name: Air Quality Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when retrieving current, 4-day forecast, or 30-day historical air quality data, including the Air Quality Index (AQI), primary pollutants, sources and effects, and related health recommendations for a specific geographic location. This skill also provides API access for generating air quality heatmap tiles for map visualization.

skills.name: Consumer Sdk For Android Android
skills.description: Use this skill when an Android developer needs to integrate a customizable, interactive map that provides real-time, consumer-facing trip progress tracking managed by Google Fleet Engine. It covers map styling, route polyline customization, and marker management for the trip.

skills.name: Consumer Sdk For Ios Ios
skills.description: Use this skill when developers need to implement real-time tracking of Fleet Engine trips on an iOS device, allowing them to display dynamic route updates, customizable vehicle and destination markers, and apply branded map styling using the Consumer SDK for iOS.

skills.name: Directions Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when calculating travel directions, distances, and estimated travel times between two or more geographic points. It enables specifying travel modes (driving, walking, transit, two-wheeled), defining intermediate waypoints, configuring traffic optimization, and setting route preferences like avoiding tolls or highways. This skill returns structured data including detailed step-by-step instructions, maneuver descriptions, and optimized route geometry via encoded polylines.

skills.name: Distance Matrix Api Web Api
skills.description: Uses the Routes API's Compute Route Matrix endpoint to calculate travel distances and estimated durations between multiple origin-destination pairs. Profiles can specify travel modes such as driving, walking, or transit for efficient matrix calculations.

skills.name: Driver Sdk For Android Android
skills.description: Use this skill when a driver needs to implement real-time, continuous tracking and status updates from an Android device to the Fleet Engine service. This includes securely reporting the vehicle's location, current route, trip waypoints, remaining distance, and ETA derived from the Navigation SDK back to the backend fleet management system.

skills.name: Driver Sdk For Ios Ios
skills.description: Use this skill when developing an iOS application that requires a driver to interact with Google Maps Platform Fleet Engine. It handles core driver operations, including reporting the vehicle's real-time location, route, waypoints, ETA, and remaining distance to Fleet Engine, ensuring accurate trip management and visibility for fleet managers and consumers.

skills.name: Elevation Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when calculating the precise altitude (elevation) of specific geographic coordinates or determining the elevation profile (change in altitude) for a multi-point path or route using the Google Maps Platform Elevation API.

skills.name: Fleet Engine Service Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when the developer manages the lifecycle of trips and vehicles, including creation, retrieval, updates, and usage reporting, for an on-demand rideshare or delivery provider using the Fleet Engine Service.

skills.name: Geocoding Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when needing to convert street addresses into precise geographic coordinates (geocoding) or translating coordinates back into human-readable addresses (reverse geocoding). Developers can refine results using viewports, regional restrictions, or retrieve detailed location information for addresses, coordinates, or Google Place IDs, including building outlines and entrances.

skills.name: Geolocation Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when needing to determine the latitude and longitude coordinates of a device based on its cell tower and Wi-Fi access point information. Developers utilize the Geolocation API to estimate location when GPS or native browser geolocation is unavailable or imprecise, typically via a secure HTTPS POST request.

skills.name: Gmp Common Api Keys
skills.description: Use this skill when the user needs to understand Google Maps Platform authentication, how to generate API keys, implement security restrictions, or use the temporary Maps Demo Key for prototyping.

skills.name: Gmp Common Grounding
skills.description: Access Google Maps geospatial data for LLM grounding via MCP tools (search, weather, routes) and the experimental Resolution API.

skills.name: Gmp Common Pricing
skills.description: Provide comprehensive guidelines on Google Maps Platform pricing, including subscription tiers, pay-as-you-go rates, volume discounts, and links to detailed cost information.

skills.name: Gmp Common Tos
skills.description: Methodical compliance review guide for Google Maps Platform Terms of Service, Acceptable Use Policy, EEA terms, AI terms, and deprecation policies.

skills.name: Gmp Framework Flutter
skills.description: This skill provides the comprehensive architecture and implementation details for developing Google Maps Platform applications using **Flutter** and the officially supported `google_maps_flutter` package. It covers map initialization, marker management, event handling, camera control, clustering, and map styling using the declarative Flutter widget model.

skills.name: Gmp Framework React
skills.description: The agent functions as an expert Google Maps Platform architect, focusing on modern web development using the React framework. This skill provides comprehensive guidance, patterns, and boilerplate code utilizing the official `@vis.gl/react-google-maps` library paired with TypeScript. It enforces the use of modern, non-deprecated Google Maps Platform APIs (like `AdvancedMarkerElement`, Places API New, and Routes API) and strictly adheres to the mandated compliance and cost-optimization checkpoints, ensuring developers build secure, performant, and future-proof mapping applications. When advising on web projects, this resource dictates the use of React/TypeScript as the default implementation path.

skills.name: Javascript Consumer Sdk Javascript
skills.description: Use this skill when implementing a customer-facing map interface that displays real-time trip progress, vehicle location, and ETA using the Google Maps Platform JavaScript Consumer SDK. It enables developers to customize map styling, manage route polylines, update driver markers, and control dynamic map features associated with trips managed by Fleet Engine.

skills.name: Map Tiles Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill to fetch 2D raster tiles (roadmap, satellite, terrain), photorealistic 3D map tiles, and Google Street View imagery for integration into custom rendering engines or applications.

skills.name: Maps 3D Sdk For Android Android
skills.description: Use this skill when a developer needs to integrate and customize the experimental photorealistic 3D map experience within an Android application using Kotlin or Java. It enables adding advanced 3D geometry, controlling the map camera path and restrictions, and responding to user interactions on the map canvas.

skills.name: Maps 3D Sdk For Ios Ios
skills.description: Use this skill when developing a native iOS application and needing to integrate interactive, photorealistic 3D map views. It covers initializing the map, adding detailed 3D content such as customizable markers, polylines, polygons, and complex 3D models, as well as managing camera movement, event handling, and UI controls for a fully immersive spatial experience.

skills.name: Maps Datasets Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when managing custom geospatial datasets for Google Maps Platform. Developers can create, update, and manage datasets by uploading data in CSV, GeoJSON, or KML formats via the Datasets API, and retrieve metadata and content for styling map visualizations.

skills.name: Maps Embed Api Javascript
skills.description: Use this skill to generate the necessary iframe HTML and URL parameters for embedding interactive Google Maps content—including standard views, directions, Street View, or place search results—directly into a web page without writing complex JavaScript code.

skills.name: Maps Javascript Api Javascript
skills.description: Use this skill to build highly performant, customizable, and interactive 2D and 3D map experiences for the web using JavaScript. Implement features like advanced markers, location search, routing/directions, geospatial data visualization, and real-time fleet tracking, adhering strictly to modern API standards like the Routes and Places (New) classes.

skills.name: Maps Sdk For Android Android
skills.description: Use this skill when developing native Android applications (Kotlin/Java) that require interactive mapping, map styling via Cloud Console, custom markers, geographical data overlays (KML, GeoJSON), Street View integration, or custom map controls and gestures.

skills.name: Maps Sdk For Ios Ios
skills.description: Use this skill when developers need to integrate an interactive, customizable map into a native iOS application using the Maps SDK for iOS (Swift/Objective-C). It covers creating map views, adding markers, shapes, custom styling, handling user interactions, and incorporating Street View functionality.

skills.name: Maps Static Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when the developer needs to generate a high-quality, non-interactive, configurable map image (PNG or JPG) using a single HTTP GET request. It allows configuring map location, size, format, style, adding markers, paths, and geometric shapes for embedding in emails, reports, or web pages where interactivity is not required.

skills.name: Navigation Sdk For Android Android
skills.description: Use this skill when integrating interactive, fully customizable, voice-guided, turn-by-turn navigation and map display features into a native Android application. It provides map rendering, advanced styling, real-time routing updates, maneuver instructions, traffic layers, custom UI controls, and support for Android Auto.

skills.name: Navigation Sdk For Ios Ios
skills.description: Use this skill to integrate interactive, turn-by-turn navigation features, real-time routing updates, UI customization, and disruption reporting/handling into an iOS application, including support for Apple CarPlay.

skills.name: Places Aggregate Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when generating SQL queries to analyze Google Places data, allowing developers to retrieve aggregated counts or specific Place IDs based on geographic boundaries and filtering criteria like place type, operating status, price level, and user ratings from BigQuery datasets.

skills.name: Places Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when searching for places, retrieving detailed information (reviews, photos, AI summaries), providing location autocomplete, or calculating route-based proximity for points of interest via the Google Maps Platform Places API (New).

skills.name: Places Insights Api Web Api
skills.description: Uses this skill when querying the Google Maps Platform Places Insights datasets in BigQuery. Developers can formulate and execute SQL queries to retrieve aggregated analytics on places, including counts by type, brand, and geographical area using specialized BigQuery functions and table schemas.

skills.name: Places Sdk For Android Android
skills.description: Use this skill when developing Android applications that require place searching, autocomplete predictions, retrieving detailed information (including ratings, photos, and reviews), or displaying AI-powered summaries of locations and nearby areas.

skills.name: Places Sdk For Ios Ios
skills.description: Use this skill when integrating advanced location intelligence and place search functionality—including autocomplete, place details, photo display, and AI-powered summaries—into an iOS mobile application using Swift or Objective-C.

skills.name: Pollen Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when developing applications that need detailed, location-based pollen forecasts, including plant sources, health recommendations, and visualization via heatmap tiles, using the Pollen Web API.

skills.name: Roads Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when cleaning raw GPS tracking data or enhancing recorded routes. Developers can snap coordinate paths to the precise road network (Snap to Roads), identify the nearest road segment to a single point (Nearest Roads), and retrieve critical segment data, including speed limits and unique Google Place IDs for accurate asset tracking and compliance.

skills.name: Route Optimization Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when optimizing multi-stop delivery or service vehicle routes. It enables the user to define complex operational constraints, including vehicle availability, capacity limits, shipment pickup/delivery time windows, and cost models, to generate a time-window-compliant, cost-effective routing plan.

skills.name: Routes Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when calculating precise routes and detailed turn-by-turn directions, optimizing stop order, specifying advanced route features like eco-friendly modes or tolls, or computing travel times and distances for a matrix of origins and destinations via the Routes API.

skills.name: Solar Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when calculating the solar potential of a specific rooftop, including panel size estimates, annual sunshine hours, carbon offset potential, and accessing GeoTIFF files for imagery and topographic data via the Solar API.

skills.name: Street View Static Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when you need to generate a static, non-interactive Google Street View image URL that can be easily embedded into web pages or emails. This skill manages parameters for location, size, field of view, and camera orientation (heading and pitch).

skills.name: Time Zone Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when developing applications that require translating a specific latitude/longitude coordinate and timestamp into the correct time zone, including calculating UTC offsets, daylight saving time (DST) information, and the corresponding CLDR ID.

skills.name: Weather Api Web Api
skills.description: Use this skill when retrieving current weather conditions, daily or hourly forecasts, 24-hour historical data, or real-time weather alerts for specified latitude and longitude coordinates via the Google Maps Platform Weather API.
